questions.prompt: When DNSFilter blocks a domain, where does the block decision actually happen?
choices: In the user's browser, after the page partially loads
At the resolver, before any TCP connection is opened
At the firewall, after the IP packet is sent
On the destination server, which refuses the request
questions.explanation: DNSFilter is a protective DNS resolver. The decision is made when the resolver answers the DNS query, before the user's machine ever opens a TCP connection.

questions.prompt: DNSFilter can allow one URL path on a domain while blocking another path on the same domain.
questions.explanation: DNSFilter operates at the DNS layer and only sees domain names, not URL paths. Per-path control is the job of a web gateway, not a DNS filter.

questions.prompt: Which of these are DNSFilter's published anycast resolver IPs?
questions.explanation: DNSFilter publishes 103.247.36.36 and 103.247.37.37 as its anycast resolver IPs. The others are Cloudflare, Google, and OpenDNS respectively.

questions.prompt: Which of the following are core areas of the DNSFilter console a frontline tech should be able to find? (Select all that apply.)
questions.explanation: Network Sites, Roaming Clients, DNS Query Log, and Filtering Policies are core. Endpoint Detection Rules are an EDR concept, not part of DNSFilter.

questions.prompt: MSP staff should confirm the organisation name in the dashboard header before making any change for a customer.
questions.explanation: The most expensive MSP mistake is editing the wrong customer's policy because the tenant switcher landed somewhere else. The header is the canonical check.

questions.prompt: What time window does the DNS Query Log show by default?
questions.explanation: The default view is the last 15 minutes. Widen it for older queries; retention tops out around 9 days depending on plan.

questions.prompt: Put the frontline triage questions in the correct order.
items: What fix does the ticket need?
questions.explanation: Each question gates the next: identify the domain, identify the reason, judge whether DNSFilter was correct, then decide the fix.

questions.prompt: An entry on a Filtering Policy's Allow list overrides any category block, threat feed, or block list for the queries that policy applies to.
questions.explanation: DNSFilter's documented hierarchy: Allow lists always win. Universal Allow or per-policy Allow override every other block path.

questions.prompt: When should a frontline tech NOT add an Allow list entry on their own? (Select all that apply.)
choices: The user is in a different customer organisation
The user is being blocked by Newly Registered Domain on a known supplier
questions.explanation: Threat-category blocks (Phishing / Malware / Botnet / Cryptomining) are escalations, not allowlist work. Cross-customer fixes also escalate. The supplier-NRD case is the classic frontline allowlist scenario.

questions.prompt: Which of these environments are NOT supported by the DNSFilter Windows Roaming Client? (Select all that apply.)
questions.explanation: DNSFilter explicitly does not support Windows Server, shared-desktop environments, or Windows 365 Cloud PC. Windows 10/11 x64 and ARM64 are all supported.

questions.prompt: A customer mandates a full-tunnel VPN, and the Roaming Client stops filtering when VPN is up. Which fix keeps both the VPN policy and DNSFilter enforcement?
choices: Uninstall the Roaming Client on VPN-using devices
Disable VPN on devices that need DNSFilter
Point the VPN's DNS resolver at 127.0.0.2 so DNS routes through the Roaming Client
Add the VPN's exit IP as a Network Site
questions.explanation: DNSFilter's documented workaround for full-tunnel VPNs is to set the VPN's DNS resolver to the loopback 127.0.0.2, which directs DNS queries through the Roaming Client even while the VPN is up.

questions.prompt: After a Roaming Client install, what counts as 'done' verification? (Select all that apply.)
choices: The DNSFilter Agent service is running
The device shows up in the DNS Query Log with recent queries
A test domain returns the DNSFilter block page
The customer sent a thank-you email
questions.explanation: Done means the dashboard agrees with the local install. Service running, queries flowing, and a test-block-page domain working end-to-end.
